pr.title: Add missing README section on diffing
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Documentation-only PR adding a missing Diffing section to .github/README.md. The new section explains that a successful initial build creates objdiff.json in the project root, tells contributors to install encounter/objdiff, set the Project directory, and use the left sidebar to select objects. It also documents that edits to source files, headers, configure.py, splits.txt, or symbols.txt trigger automatic rebuilds, and adds an objdiff screenshot asset.
what_changed: Inserted a new top-level README section titled 'Diffing' after the Make build instructions and before the Contributing section.
Documented that once the initial build succeeds, objdiff.json should be present in the project root.
Directed contributors to download the latest release of encounter/objdiff from https://github.com/encounter/objdiff.
Explained that in objdiff project settings, contributors should set 'Project directory' and expect the configuration to load automatically.
Explained basic objdiff usage: select an object from the left sidebar to begin diffing.
Listed files whose changes trigger automatic rebuilds: source files, headers, configure.py, splits.txt, and symbols.txt.
Added a README image reference to assets/objdiff.png and included the new binary screenshot asset.
smart_moves: Placed diffing instructions directly in the onboarding README flow, immediately after build setup, matching the point where a contributor would next need objdiff.
Kept the instructions short and operational: build first, locate/use objdiff.json implicitly through project directory selection, then select an object.
Called out automatic rebuild triggers, which helps contributors understand why objdiff updates after source/header/config/split/symbol edits.
Used a screenshot to reduce ambiguity for first-time objdiff users.
decomp_lessons: For decomp onboarding, document the path from a successful build to visual diffing; contributors need to know when objdiff.json is generated and how objdiff consumes it.
When a repo has generated objdiff configuration, contributors should generally point objdiff at the project directory rather than manually configuring individual objects.
Documentation should explicitly mention that changes to source files, headers, configure.py, splits.txt, and symbols.txt can affect rebuild/diff behavior.
Small README additions can remove friction in the decomp loop: edit code, rebuild automatically, inspect object diff.
naming_conventions: README section title uses plain gerund form: 'Diffing'.
Tool/config names are written literally as 'objdiff', 'objdiff.json', 'configure.py', 'splits.txt', and 'symbols.txt'.
The screenshot asset is named descriptively after the tool: .github/assets/objdiff.png.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level or matching-code tactic was changed in this PR.

The documented workflow supports matching work by guiding contributors to use objdiff after building and by noting automatic rebuilds after source, header, configure.py, splits.txt, or symbols.txt changes.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find PRs that document objdiff setup or objdiff.json behavior
Find README onboarding changes around building, diffing, and contributing
Find documentation mentioning automatic rebuild triggers for configure.py, splits.txt, or symbols.txt
